summary: Good‑quality audience recording with crisp sound (though a bit slow) captures an energetic 4/2/90 show highlighted by The Weight, Let It Grow, Foolish Heart and a powerful Last Time.
review: The audience tape delivers clear, full‑bodied audio that puts the crowd ambience front‑and‑center, even if the playback speed is slightly low. Musically the concert shines – The Weight appears for the second time, Let It Grow and Foolish Heart soar, and the band rides a wave of excitement through Last Time and Death Don't Have No Mercy. A few reviewers note a technically challenged Other One, but overall the performance is praised as a peak‑era Dead show.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical performance (standout songs and high energy) combined with generally good sound quality; only minor issues (pitch slowdown, one weak Other One) keep it from a perfect score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp and full‑presence mix with noticeable crowd reverb; reviewers praise clarity but several note the tape plays about a quarter‑tone slow, suggesting a pitch/frequency correction would improve it.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second‑night energy builds from a quirky opener into a tight first set, then launches into a song‑focused second set that feels both jazzy and psychedelic – a smooth, high‑groove arc with the crowd riding every transition.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Sweet lick highlighted as 'never heard again' and many reviewers call Foolish Heart an all‑Jerry moment.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass noted for taking over from the start; strong presence throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboards described as ‘shakin’ like Ray Charles’, adding vivid color to the mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drum capture praised for unusual accuracy and precision in an audience source.
